mentions.raw_name: Arya Stark (POV; aliases: Weasel, Arry, Arya Underfoot, Lumpyhead, Horseface, Nan, Nymeria)
The black bear (caged animal, used in execution)
House Bolton (flayed man sigil; the Dreadfort)
House Glover (silver mailed fist sigil; Deepwood Motte)
House Karstark (white sun on black sigil)
Gold cloaks (referenced — queen sent them after Gendry)
The old gods (Arya prays at the heart tree; Jaqen swears by "the old gods beyond count")
The seven new gods (Jaqen swears by them)
Gods of sea and air (Jaqen's oath)
Him of fire (Jaqen's oath — R'hllor implied)
The Faith of the Seven (High Septon mentioned hypothetically)
Northmen (the captured soldiers; "from the wild north up where that Wall is")
Lorathi (Jaqen described as "the Lorathi"; from the Free City of Lorath)
Iron coin (given by Jaqen to Arya; "a coin of great value")
Cell key (hung on wall hook above table)
Banners: lion of Lannister, black manticore of Lorch, flayed man of Dreadfort, direwolf of Stark
Arya's kill list prayer (whispered names to her pillow: Joffrey, Cersei, Ser Ilyn, Ser Meryn, the Hound, and others)

mentions.raw_name: Old Nan's stories about grumkins and magic wishes
mentions.section: Raw Entity List > In-world Texts & Songs

mentions.raw_name: Face-changing (Jaqen passes hand down his face and transforms his appearance completely)
Jaqen's three deaths / death debt ("Three lives were snatched from a god. Three lives must be repaid.")
Weese's dog turning on him (Arya attributes to dark magic)
Arya's identity concealment / ghost imagery ("I am the ghost in Harrenhal")
War of the Five Kings (implicit context — Robb's men captured; Bolton and Tywin at war; Harrenhal changes hands)
Castellan of Harrenhal (held by Ser Amory Lorch, appointed by Lord Tywin)
Lord of the Dreadfort (Roose Bolton's title, announced by his squire)
Cupbearer (Arya's new role under Roose Bolton)
Lord commander (Glover identified as lord commander of the captured force)
Leeches (Bolton's belief in frequent leechings as secret of long life)
"Weasel soup" (Shagwell's name for the dungeon-break trick, spread through castle)
